topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the login details tucked inside an old, forgotten charging cable. My stomach plummeted as I typed them in, expecting anything but this. The screen flickered, and there it was: 'ShadowStarlight99'. My daughter, Maya, had a secret digital persona. A wave of hot flush crept up my neck. I immediately slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet living room. I had to pretend I hadn't seen anything, to act like this discovery hadn't unsettled the carefully constructed calm of my evening.

[story 2]

The notification popped up on my phone while I was making coffee. It was a direct message from an unknown user, and the content made my hands tremble slightly. I followed the link, and there, a curated feed of Maya’s life existed—a world completely separate from the polite, bookish girl I knew. A tight knot formed in my chest, making it difficult to breathe evenly. I quickly minimized the browser window, my gaze darting around the room as if searching for an escape route. I felt a distinct tightening around my jaw as I tried to process the sheer scope of what I’d stumbled upon.

[story 3]

I caught my daughter scrolling through her phone, and the screen illuminated a profile picture I didn't recognize. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled me closer. When I saw the username—something cryptic and undeniably public—a sudden, overwhelming need to retreat washed over me. I mumbled an excuse about needing to check the mail and backed away slowly, my feet feeling heavy on the hardwood floor. Every muscle in my body seemed to tense up; I desperately wished the floor would swallow me whole. The silence in the house suddenly felt deafening, filled only with the frantic drumming of my own pulse.
